content.text: I’m adding a controller-level behavior test rather than only another UI assertion. The useful check is whether a healthy live completion avoids the extra timeline fetch, because that is the thing that causes the visual reset.

content.text:
I’m going with two focused controller tests:
- healthy live completion should not refetch the timeline
- completion without meaningful live activity should still do the snapshot catch-up

That directly covers the stability/no-flash policy instead of only UI symptoms.

---
name: codex-mobile-live-e2e
description: Run and debug live Android-emulator E2E tests for the codex-mobile-companion repository when the task requires a real local bridge, adb port reversal, flutter drive integration tests, or bridge/snapshot diagnosis for Claude Code or Codex thread flows. Use for cases like Claude title generation, Claude approval/file-change timeline checks, Codex thread creation hanging in loading, or any request to verify mobile behavior against the local workspace bridge instead of the app-bundled bridge.
---

# Codex Mobile Live E2E

Run the real bridge-backed Android emulator flows for `codex-mobile-companion` without re-deriving the port, environment, and debugging setup each time.

## Workflow

1. Confirm the workspace is `/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion` unless the user explicitly says otherwise.
2. Use `scripts/start_local_bridge.sh` to run the workspace bridge on `127.0.0.1:3310`.
3. Use `scripts/run_live_test.sh <integration target>` to:
   `adb reverse tcp:3310 tcp:3310`
   force-stop the Android app for a clean run
   launch `flutter drive` with the correct `--dart-define` pair for the target
4. If the test fails or hangs, inspect the bridge snapshot and upstream rollout artifacts before changing code.
5. After bridge-side code changes, rebuild and rerun the same live target that reproduced the issue.

## Targets

- Claude title generation:
  `apps/mobile/integration_test/live_claude_code_title_generation_test.dart`
- Claude approval edit and file-change surfacing:
  `apps/mobile/integration_test/live_claude_code_approval_edit_test.dart`
- Codex draft creation and first-turn completion:
  `apps/mobile/integration_test/live_codex_thread_creation_test.dart`

For the exact commands and env wiring, read [live-tests.md](./references/live-tests.md).

## Debugging

- Prefer the local bridge on `3310` when validating workspace changes. Do not assume the app-bundled bridge on `3110` is using the code you just edited.
- If a thread appears stuck, query:
  `GET /threads`
  `GET /threads/<thread_id>/snapshot`
  `GET /threads/<thread_id>/history`
- If bridge state disagrees with the app, inspect `~/.codex/session_index.jsonl` and the matching `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-...jsonl` file.
- For Codex turn issues, compare:
  the bridge snapshot status and entries
  the rollout file's `event_msg` / `response_item` records
- If `flutter drive` hangs after install or leaves a dirty app process behind, force-stop `com.example.vibe_bridge` and rerun without `--keep-app-running`.

## Bridge Notes

- `scripts/start_local_bridge.sh` rebuilds `bridge-server` and runs it from the workspace.
- `scripts/run_live_test.sh` selects the right `--dart-define` names for Claude vs Codex live targets.
- If a new live target is added, extend the case statement in `scripts/run_live_test.sh`.

## Validation

- Validate bridge-only fixes with targeted Rust tests first.
- Then rerun the live target that exposed the bug.
- If the emulator harness is flaky, verify the fix directly against the same live bridge by creating a fresh thread and polling `/threads/<thread_id>/snapshot`.

entries.summary: I’ve confirmed the flash source in the controller: completion currently forces a timeline snapshot refresh for every non-running status. I’...

content.text: I’ve confirmed the flash source in the controller: completion currently forces a timeline snapshot refresh for every non-running status. I’m pulling the rest of the controller helpers and the scripted test bridge so I can gate that refresh cleanly and cover it with tests before rerunning Samsung.

payload.delta: I’ve confirmed the flash source in the controller: completion currently forces a timeline snapshot refresh for every non-running status. I’m pulling the rest of the controller helpers and the scripted test bridge so I can gate that refresh cleanly and cover it with tests before rerunning Samsung.

entries.summary: I’ve narrowed the safe fix: detail refresh stays, snapshot refresh becomes a fallback only when the live turn looked unhealthy. I’m checkin...

content.text: I’ve narrowed the safe fix: detail refresh stays, snapshot refresh becomes a fallback only when the live turn looked unhealthy. I’m checking the activity-item mapping now so the controller can distinguish “healthy live streaming happened” from “we need a catch-up snapshot.”

payload.delta: I’ve narrowed the safe fix: detail refresh stays, snapshot refresh becomes a fallback only when the live turn looked unhealthy. I’m checking the activity-item mapping now so the controller can distinguish “healthy live streaming happened” from “we need a catch-up snapshot.”

entries.summary: I’m checking the status-change reasons before I wire the fallback. That part matters because Codex also emits non-turn metadata lifecycle e...

content.text: I’m checking the status-change reasons before I wire the fallback. That part matters because Codex also emits non-turn metadata lifecycle events, and I only want the expensive snapshot refresh on actual turn-terminal states.

payload.delta: I’m checking the status-change reasons before I wire the fallback. That part matters because Codex also emits non-turn metadata lifecycle events, and I only want the expensive snapshot refresh on actual turn-terminal states.

entries.summary: I’m editing the controller now. The change is narrow: keep the metadata refresh on completion, but stop replacing the visible timeline unle...

content.text: I’m editing the controller now. The change is narrow: keep the metadata refresh on completion, but stop replacing the visible timeline unless the turn finished in a suspicious state. Then I’ll add the two regression tests that pin both sides of that behavior.
